2606.22959.title: The Impact of VAE Design on Latent Pose Representations for Diffusion-based Sign Language Production
2606.22959.paper_summary: Latent diffusion approaches to sign language production (SLP) rely on an initial stage that learns an encoding of sign pose sequences, enabling generative modeling in the resulting latent space. The autoencoder used in this stage is typically evaluated in terms of reconstruction quality using geometric metrics common in SLP. While informative, these metrics do not fully capture latent space properties that may influence the training and performance of the downstream generative model. In this work, we investigate how architectural and training objective design choices in a variational autoencoder (VAE) for sign pose encoding affect latent space structure, and how these differences translate into the performance of a latent diffusion model for text-to-sign generation. Our experiments on Phoenix14T dataset show that variations in generative performance, measured through back-translation BLEU scores, can sometimes be better explained by differences in latent space properties than by VAE reconstruction accuracy alone.
2606.22959.paper_summary_zh: 潛在擴散方法在手語生成（SLP）中依賴於一個初始階段，該階段學習手勢姿勢序列的編碼，從而使得在生成的潛在空間中進行建模成為可能。這一階段使用的自編碼器通常通過使用在SLP中常見的幾何指標來評估重建質量。雖然這些指標提供了有用的信息，但並未完全捕捉到可能影響下游生成模型訓練和性能的潛在空間特性。在本研究中，我們探討了在手勢姿勢編碼的變分自編碼器（VAE）中，架構和訓練目標設計選擇如何影響潛在空間結構，以及這些差異如何轉化為文本到手勢生成的潛在擴散模型的性能。我們在Phoenix14T數據集上的實驗顯示，通過回譯BLEU分數測量的生成性能變化，有時可以更好地通過潛在空間特性的差異來解釋，而不僅僅是VAE重建準確性。
2606.22959.author: Guilhem Fauré et.al.
2606.22959.authors: Guilhem Fauré, Mostafa Sadeghi, Sam Bigeard, Slim Ouni
